methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
ZeroAvia, a Series C transportation company founded by Valery Miftakhov, has raised $150M+ to date. The relevant comp universe consists of seven private transportation firms including Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft, with no public comparables available. Traditional peer-median valuation was not viable given insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage peers meeting minimum clustering thresholds (fewer than three datapoints within applicable filters).

Stage-progression methodology represents the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised of $150M as a proxy base value and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement yields an implied valuation of $255M. This method directly reflects observed progression patterns in early-stage venture capital despite limited narrow peer density.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $217M to $293M (representing ±15% sensitivity bands around the $255M median), with $255M as the point estimate. The primary valuation uncertainty stems from the absence of sufficiently similar-staged and sized private comparables in transportation, limiting triangulation to a single methodological anchor. Investor confidence in this range depends materially on conviction regarding ZeroAvia's execution trajectory and capital efficiency relative to the broader frontier-transport peer set, which remains difficult to assess without multi-method corroboration.
